summary: This lecture discusses a hadith from Sahih Muslim about Ad-Dajjal (the one-eyed liar), a major sign of the Day of Judgment warned about by all prophets. The speaker clarifies the description of Ad-Dajjal as literally one-eyed (having two eyes with one deficient), not metaphorical references to television or internet. The lecture emphasizes that Ad-Dajjal is described as a human being with specific physical characteristics including being young, muscular, with coarse curly hair, and having text written between his eyes. The discussion then pivots to theological understanding of Allah's attributes, particularly His eyes, explaining that negative descriptions of Allah (what He is not) only constitute praise when their positive opposites are implied. The speaker clarifies that Allah's attributes like sight, hearing, and having eyes are real but unfathomable to human comprehension.
suggested_title: Understanding Ad-Dajjal's Description and the Attributes of Allah
hadith_chapter: Book of Al-Fitan (Trials and Tribulations), Chapter on Ad-Dajjal
